title: opt_mem pass is not respecting (* keep *)
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.51+85 (git sha1 1113c8c95, g++ 11.4.0-1ubuntu1~22.04 -fPIC -O3)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

With the following module:

```verilog
module comb_mem_d1 #(
    parameter WIDTH = 32,
    parameter SIZE = 16,
    parameter IDX_SIZE = 4
) (
   input wire                logic [IDX_SIZE-1:0] addr0,
   input wire                logic [ WIDTH-1:0] write_data,
   input wire                logic write_en,
   input wire                logic clk,
   input wire                logic reset,
   (* keep *)output                    logic [ WIDTH-1:0] read_data,
);

  (* keep *)logic [WIDTH-1:0] mem[SIZE-1:0];

  /* verilator lint_off WIDTH */
  assign read_data = mem[addr0];

  always_ff @(posedge clk) begin
    if (!reset && write_en)
      mem[addr0] <= write_data;
  end
endmodule

module test();
  logic [3:0] addr0;
  logic [31:0] write_data;
  logic write_en;
  logic clk;
  logic reset;
  logic [31:0] read_data1;
  logic [31:0] read_data2;
  logic done;

  assign write_en = 1;

  (* keep=1 *)
  comb_mem_d1 #(
    .WIDTH(32),
    .SIZE(16),
    .IDX_SIZE(4)
  ) data1 (
    .addr0(addr0),
    .write_data(),
    .write_en(),
    .clk(clk),
    .reset(reset),
    .read_data(read_data1),
  );

  (* keep=1 *)
  comb_mem_d1 #(
      .WIDTH(32),
      .SIZE(16),
      .IDX_SIZE(4)
  ) data2 (
      .addr0(addr0),
      .write_data(),
      .write_en(),
      .clk(clk),
      .reset(reset),
      .read_data(read_data2),
  );

  (* keep=1 *)
  comb_mem_d1 #(
      .WIDTH(32),
      .SIZE(16),
      .IDX_SIZE(4)
  ) data3 (
      .addr0(addr0),
      .write_data(write_data),
      .write_en(write_en),
      .clk(clk),
      .reset(reset),
      .read_data()
  );

  assign write_data = read_data1 + read_data2;

endmodule

```

And running the following command:
```
read_verilog -sv example.sv
hierarchy -auto-top; flatten; proc; opt -full
memory -nomap
stat
```

### Expected Behavior

After calling the stat command, I should still see 3 `$memwr_v2` cell.

### Actual Behavior

I am only left with 1 `$memwr_v2` cell.

```
=== test ===

   Number of wires:                 26
   Number of wire bits:            317
   Number of public wires:          26
   Number of public wire bits:     317
   Number of ports:                  0
   Number of port bits:              0
   Number of memories:               0
   Number of memory bits:            0
   Number of processes:              0
   Number of cells:                  5
     $add                            1
     $mem_v2                         1
     $scopeinfo                      3
```

hierarchy -auto-top; flatten; proc; opt -full
